# openshift Provider

Provider for airgapped on-premises OpenShift deployments on bare metal via the Assisted Installer API.



## Example Usage

### Connected — Red Hat hosted Assisted Installer (api.openshift.com)

```hcl
provider "openshift" {
  assisted_service_url   = "https://api.openshift.com"
  assisted_offline_token = var.offline_token
}
```

### Self-hosted Assisted Installer on a bastion (airgapped)

Deploy the service first, then feed its output URL back into the provider. Because the provider block is evaluated before resources, use the per-resource `assisted_service_url` override on early resources, or split into two Terraform workspaces (one that provisions the bastion service, one that uses it).

```hcl
resource "openshift_assisted_service" "bastion" {
  bastion_host     = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_ssh_key  = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")
  service_base_url = "http://bastion.example.internal:8090"
}

provider "openshift" {
  # No token needed — the self-hosted service is unauthenticated by default.
  assisted_service_url = openshift_assisted_service.bastion.api_url
}
```

### With kubeconfig for post-install resources

Required when managing `openshift_machine_config`, `openshift_subscription`, or `openshift_image_mirror` resources against an already-installed cluster.

```hcl
provider "openshift" {
  assisted_service_url   = "https://api.openshift.com"
  assisted_offline_token = var.offline_token
  kubeconfig             = "/home/ops/.kube/prod-cluster"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `assisted_offline_token` (String, Sensitive) Red Hat offline token (from console.redhat.com/openshift/token). Automatically exchanged for a bearer token and refreshed on expiry. Mutually exclusive with assisted_service_token.
- `assisted_service_token` (String, Sensitive) Static bearer token for the Assisted Installer API. Mutually exclusive with assisted_offline_token.
- `assisted_service_url` (String) Base URL of the Assisted Installer service, e.g. https://api.openshift.com. Can also be set per-resource.
- `kubeconfig` (String) Path to kubeconfig for post-install resources (machine_config, subscription, etc.). Defaults to KUBECONFIG env var.
- `oc_binary` (String) Path to oc binary used by image_mirror. Defaults to 'oc' on PATH.
